title: Xanthisma coloradoense
intro: Xanthisma coloradoense, also known as Plains Blacksam or Plains Yarrow, is a perennial plant species in the sunflower family native to the Great Plains region of North America. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which can help soothe wounds and reduce pain. The bioactive constituents of Xanthisma coloradoense include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant are tinctures and infusions, which are used to treat a variety of ailments, including digestive issues and skin problems.
taxonomy: Xanthisma coloradoense is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is a member of the genus Xanthisma, which is a small group of plants native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. The genus Xanthisma is closely related to the genus Hymenopappus and is often included in the same group. Xanthisma coloradoense is typically considered to be a perennial or annual plant, depending on the specific environmental conditions. This classification can help scientists understand the plant's evolution and relationships with other species.
uses: Xanthisma coloradoense, also known as Plains Blue Sanvitalia, is a plant native to the Great Plains region of North America.
